comment.body:
Have you guys read my post about market theories? there i cover mean reversion in short.
think of a simple normal distribution.
the lvls give you the ranges with probabilities assuming normal distribution, that the price will move in the range, means, rejecting the line. 
IV is the volatility that the market bets on. It is the risk premium in the option price. I take it and project the distribution that the priced IV covers.

So the mean reversion lvls give you idea about the price extremes where are the lvls where the participants expect reversion. 
You can use Kelly to size the probability according to the lvl values.

Risk lvl is momentum confirmer.
comment.raw_body:
